relationships.description: NIST RMF Prepare step maps to NIST CSF Govern function. Both establish organizational risk management strategy, roles, and risk tolerance.
NIST RMF Risk Management Strategy task maps to NIST CSF Risk Management Strategy category.
NIST RMF organization-level Risk Assessment task maps to NIST CSF Risk Assessment category.
NIST RMF Continuous Monitoring Strategy task maps to NIST CSF Continuous Monitoring category.
NIST RMF Categorize step maps to NIST CSF Identify function. Categorizing systems by impact aligns with understanding organizational cybersecurity risks.
NIST RMF System Description task maps to NIST CSF Asset Management. Documenting system characteristics is a form of asset management.
NIST RMF Select step maps to NIST SP 800-53 Planning family. RMF Select drives the selection and tailoring of 800-53 controls.
NIST RMF Select step relates to NIST CSF Protect function. Selecting controls provides the safeguards that implement protection.
NIST RMF Implement step maps to NIST CSF Protect function. Implementing controls directly provides the protective safeguards.
NIST RMF Assess step maps to NIST SP 800-53 Assessment, Authorization, and Monitoring family.
NIST RMF Authorize step maps to NIST SP 800-53 Assessment, Authorization, and Monitoring family for authorization decisions.
NIST RMF Monitor step maps to NIST CSF Detect function. Ongoing monitoring enables detection of adverse events.
NIST RMF Monitor step relates to NIST CSF Improvement category. Continuous monitoring drives identification of improvements.
NIST RMF Prepare step maps to ISO 31000 Framework Design. Both establish the organizational context and approach for risk management.
NIST RMF Risk Management Roles task relates to ISO 31000 Leadership and commitment. Assigning roles requires leadership engagement.
NIST RMF Categorize step maps to ISO 31000 Scope, context and criteria. Categorization establishes the scope and context for risk management.
NIST RMF Risk Analysis and Determination task maps to ISO 31000 Risk Analysis process step.
NIST RMF Risk Response task maps to ISO 31000 Risk Treatment. Both address selecting and implementing risk response options.
NIST RMF Monitor step maps to ISO 31000 Monitoring and review process step.
NIST RMF Assess step maps to ISO 27000 Performance evaluation. Both involve assessing the effectiveness of security controls.
NIST RMF Organizationally-Tailored Control Baselines maps to NIST SP 800-53 Program Management family for baseline establishment.
NIST RMF Prepare step maps to NIST AI RMF Govern function. Both establish organizational risk posture, roles, strategy, and risk tolerance before system-level activities begin.
NIST RMF Categorize step maps to NIST AI RMF Map function. Both contextualize systems, assess impact levels, and characterize how the system will be used.
NIST RMF Select step maps to NIST AI RMF Manage function. RMF selects and tailors controls; AI RMF plans and implements risk treatments.
NIST RMF Assess step maps to NIST AI RMF Measure function. Both evaluate whether controls and safeguards are implemented correctly and producing desired outcomes.
NIST RMF Authorize step relates to NIST AI RMF Govern function. Authorization is a governance decision requiring senior officials to accept risk.
NIST RMF Monitor step maps to NIST AI RMF Measure function. Both track ongoing risk posture and control effectiveness over time.
RMF Risk Management Roles (P-1) maps to AI RMF Accountability Structures (GOVERN 2). Both establish who is responsible for risk management activities.
RMF Risk Management Strategy (P-2) maps to AI RMF Organizational Risk Tolerances (GOVERN 4). Both define risk appetite and tolerance levels.
RMF Risk Assessment — Organization (P-3) maps to AI RMF Likelihood and Impact Assessment (MAP 5). Both assess organizational risk through likelihood and severity estimation.
RMF Common Control Identification (P-5) relates to AI RMF Third-Party Risk Management (GOVERN 5). Common controls often involve third-party and shared services.
RMF Continuous Monitoring Strategy — Organization (P-7) maps to AI RMF Tracking Mechanisms (MEASURE 3). Both establish strategies for ongoing tracking of risk indicators.
RMF System Stakeholders (P-9) maps to AI RMF Interdisciplinary AI Actors (MAP 2). Both identify stakeholders with interest in system design, development, and operation.
RMF Risk Assessment — System (P-14) maps to AI RMF Risks and Impacts (MAP 4). Both conduct system-level risk assessment identifying internal and external risks.
RMF Requirements Definition (P-15) relates to AI RMF Intended Purpose and Context (MAP 1). Defining security requirements aligns with documenting the AI system's intended purpose.
RMF Security Categorization (C-2) relates to AI RMF Likelihood and Impact Assessment (MAP 5). Security categorization is a form of impact-based assessment.
RMF Control Selection (S-1) maps to AI RMF Risk Prioritization (MANAGE 1). Both prioritize risks and select appropriate controls or treatments.
RMF Control Assessment (A-3) maps to AI RMF Trustworthiness Evaluation (MEASURE 2). Both evaluate whether safeguards meet their intended outcomes.
RMF Remediation Actions (A-5) maps to AI RMF Risk Treatment Strategies (MANAGE 2). Both plan and implement corrective actions for identified deficiencies.
RMF Plan of Action and Milestones (A-6) relates to AI RMF Risk Prioritization (MANAGE 1). Both prioritize risk remediation based on assessment findings.
RMF Risk Analysis and Determination (R-2) maps to AI RMF Risk Prioritization (MANAGE 1). Both analyze risk to determine if it is acceptable.
RMF Risk Response (R-3) maps to AI RMF Risk Response and Recovery (MANAGE 3). Both implement preferred courses of action in response to determined risks.
RMF System and Environment Changes (M-1) maps to AI RMF Tracking Mechanisms (MEASURE 3). Both monitor changes that impact the risk posture.
RMF Ongoing Assessments (M-2) maps to AI RMF Feedback and Evaluation (MEASURE 4). Both conduct periodic reassessment and incorporate lessons learned.
RMF Ongoing Risk Response (M-3) maps to AI RMF Risk Response and Recovery (MANAGE 3). Both respond to evolving risks with predefined response and recovery plans.
RMF System Disposal (M-7) maps to AI RMF Decommissioning Procedures (MANAGE 2.3). Both address end-of-life activities when a system is removed from operation.
RMF Risk Management Roles (P-1) maps to AI RMF Roles and Responsibilities (GOVERN 2.1). Both assign individuals to specific risk management roles.
RMF Risk Management Strategy (P-2) maps to AI RMF Define AI Risk Tolerances (GOVERN 4.1). Both establish risk tolerance as part of organizational risk strategy.
RMF Risk Assessment — Organization (P-3) maps to AI RMF Estimate Likelihood and Severity (MAP 5.1). Both assess risk through likelihood and impact estimation.
RMF Common Control Identification (P-5) relates to AI RMF Third-Party AI Risk Policies (GOVERN 5.1). Common controls often involve shared services requiring third-party risk policies.
RMF Continuous Monitoring Strategy — Organization (P-7) maps to AI RMF Apply Risk Tracking (MEASURE 3.1). Both establish tracking and monitoring mechanisms.
RMF System Stakeholders (P-9) maps to AI RMF Diverse Stakeholder Participation (MAP 2.2). Both ensure relevant stakeholders are identified and engaged.
RMF Risk Assessment — System (P-14) maps to AI RMF Map Internal Risks (MAP 4.2). Both conduct system-level identification of internal risks.
RMF Requirements Definition (P-15) relates to AI RMF Define Intended Purpose (MAP 1.1). Security requirements should align with the AI system's documented intended purpose.
RMF Continuous Monitoring Strategy — System (S-5) maps to AI RMF Monitor Performance Over Time (MEASURE 3.2). Both monitor system-level performance and control effectiveness over time.
RMF Control Assessment (A-3) relates to AI RMF Test Validity and Reliability (MEASURE 2.1). Control assessment includes testing whether AI safeguards produce valid and reliable outcomes.
RMF Remediation Actions (A-5) maps to AI RMF Plan Risk Treatment Strategies (MANAGE 2.1). Both plan and execute treatment strategies for identified deficiencies.
RMF Risk Response (R-3) maps to AI RMF Pre-Defined Response Plans (MANAGE 3.1). Both implement predefined response plans in response to risk determinations.
RMF Ongoing Risk Response (M-3) relates to AI RMF Recovery Plans for AI Incidents (MANAGE 3.3). Ongoing risk response includes recovery planning for AI-specific incidents.
RMF Organizationally-Tailored Control Baselines (P-4) relates to AI RMF Policies and Procedures (GOVERN 6). Both establish and maintain organizational policies for risk management.
RMF Documentation of Planned Controls (S-4) maps to AI RMF Document Risk Treatments (MANAGE 2.4). Both require documenting planned control implementations and risk treatments.
RMF Authorization Reporting (R-5) maps to AI RMF Document and Communicate Residual Risk (MANAGE 4.1). Both report remaining risk and authorization decisions to relevant stakeholders.
RMF Security and Privacy Reporting (M-5) relates to AI RMF Document and Communicate Residual Risk (MANAGE 4.1). Both communicate ongoing risk posture to organizational officials.
RMF Control Implementation (I-1) relates to AI RMF Plan Risk Treatment Strategies (MANAGE 2.1). Implementing controls is a form of executing risk treatment strategies.
RMF Assessment Reports (A-4) maps to AI RMF Residual Risk Communication (MANAGE 4). Both produce documentation of assessment findings and residual risk for decision-makers.
RMF Risk Management Roles (P-1) relates to AI RMF AI Risk Management Accountability (GOVERN 1.7). Assigning risk management roles establishes accountability for AI risk management.
RMF Risk Assessment — Organization (P-3) maps to AI RMF AI Risk Mapped to Organizational Risk (GOVERN 1.3). Both integrate system-specific risk assessment into broader enterprise risk management.
